key_insights.insight: Accumulating 'micro stress doses' throughout the morning can significantly lower your resilience and make you more easily triggered later in the day.
key_insights.quote: Many people leave the house in the morning having already accumulated about 15 micro stress doses.So they're already a lot closer to their threshold than they would have otherwise been,which means they've got less resilience.

key_insights.insight: Morning routines are valuable as they reduce the exposure to micro stress doses, building resilience for the day.
key_insights.quote: Why I think morning routines can be so valuable is they can reduce how many micro stress doses you're exposed to first thing in the morning so you are going intoyour day with much more headroom and much more resilience

key_insights.insight: A simple, effective morning routine includes mindfulness, movement, and mindset exercises to improve overall well-being.
key_insights.quote: I think a complete morning routine for me has got these three M's, mindfulness, movement, mindset.

key_insights.insight: A longer out-breath helps to lower your body's stress response and activate its relaxation response.
key_insights.quote: Anytime your out-breath is longer than your in breath,you help to lower your body's stress response and activate its relaxation response.

key_insights.insight: Affirmations, short, positive statements, have research-backed benefits, including improved performance in undergraduate students.
key_insights.quote: Undergraduate students who didaffirmations before their exam perform better, right? You know, how you program your mind matters.

key_insights.insight: Making a behavior easy to do is a key factor in ensuring its consistency.
key_insights.quote: If you make something easy, you will do it.

key_insights.insight: Attaching a new behavior to an existing habit is more effective than relying on reminders or motivation alone.
key_insights.quote: If you stick on your new behavior onto an existing habit, it's much more likely to happen.

key_insights.insight: Having visible cues like kettlebells in the kitchen serves as a constant trigger for exercise.
key_insights.quote: We need to constantly trigger...So as I'm making the coffee, I can see it. It's looking at me.

key_insights.insight: Practicing self-compassion is vital for overall health, happiness, and success.
key_insights.quote: People who are more compassionate to themselves, they're healthier, they're happier,they're more successful at work.

actionable_advice.advice: Implement a 5-minute morning routine consisting of mindfulness, movement and mindset exercises.
actionable_advice.how_to: One minute of 3-4-5 breathing (inhale for 3, hold for 4, exhale for 5), two minutes of yoga, and two minutes of affirmations.

actionable_advice.advice: Make a desired behavior easy by removing barriers to entry.
actionable_advice.how_to: Prepare workout clothes the night before, keep exercise equipment visible, or choose a nearby gym.

actionable_advice.advice: Anchor a new behavior to an existing habit.
actionable_advice.how_to: Do 5 minutes of exercise while you are waiting for your coffee to brew in the morning.

actionable_advice.advice: Incorporate positive affirmations into your routine.
actionable_advice.how_to: Hold hands with your child and repeat “I’m happy, I’m calm, I’m stress-free” for a minute.

memorable_stories: A patient with skin issues significantly improved her condition by implementing a 5-minute morning routine.
The guest's daughter joins him during his morning routine, adapting his mindfulness and mindset practices.
contrarian_claims: That beating yourself up is an effective, long term strategy for success is a myth and a short term win and long term failure.
Motivation is unreliable and should not be relied upon when planning habits and behaviors. Instead behaviors should be anchored to existing habits.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 72 - Why You Need to Care About Your Morning Routine: Dr Rangan Chatterjee
